page: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dalsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is provisioned with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also reaching the top 10%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU extended instructions scoring in the average range. Memory bandwidth remains average, registering 107.39 GB/sec for large-block read operations. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation, while medium and large LLM inference tasks fall into the average tier.

The D32als_v7 offers a compute-heavy profile with a constrained memory footprint, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high clock speeds but do not demand massive memory allocations. The primary tradeoff is the low 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage. This configuration is suitable for compute-bound tasks, batch processing, single-threaded applications, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Dalsv7 family, featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Configured with 16 physical cores and 64.0 GB of RAM, it provides a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the D32als_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded decompression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM text generation. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth fall within the average tier. This hardware profile presents a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is suited for batch processing, web serving, and single-threaded applications that do not require local disk storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for memory-light, compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 64 GB RAM, offering top-tier single-core CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 server featuring high clock-speed AMD EPYC processing and a streamlined memory footprint for dedicated single-core efficiency.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Lacks local storage and GPU resources, relying on external storage options
Suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
